evals.name: memory-palace-explicit-6-dishes
assertions: All 6 problems get a dish-card markdown file under '03 - Dishes - Problems/' with an LC-numbered filename
An interactive HTML memory-palace file is produced under '04 - Meal Plans - Practice/'
The palace renders a flip-card for every one of the 6 problems, each back showing that problem's code
The palace organizes dishes into at least 2 named, themed meal/room sections (e.g. a hashing room and a stack room)
A rhyming mnemonic poem with at least 6 dish lines appears, and it is present in BOTH the palace and the meal note
A meal note links every dish card, embeds the poem, and points to the palace HTML file
Every dish has a distinct exaggerated edible image name plus a one-line mnemonic, not a literal restatement of the problem
Stack problems (Valid Parentheses, Min Stack, RPN) map to [[Stack Pancake Stack]] and the set/hash problems map to an appropriate vault recipe in card frontmatter
Each dish card preserves the user's actual code verbatim (e.g. '^=' in Single Number, 'int(b/a)' in RPN)

evals.name: interview-recall-4-mixed
assertions: All 4 problems get a dish-card markdown file under '03 - Dishes - Problems/' with an LC-numbered filename
An interactive HTML memory-palace file is produced under '04 - Meal Plans - Practice/'
The palace renders a flip-card for every one of the 4 problems, each back showing that problem's code
The palace organizes dishes into named, themed meal/room sections (the three two-pointer problems should cluster apart from the running-min scan)
A rhyming mnemonic poem with at least 4 dish lines appears, and it is present in BOTH the palace and the meal note
A meal note links every dish card, embeds the poem, and points to the palace HTML file
Every dish has a distinct exaggerated edible image name plus a one-line mnemonic, not a literal restatement of the problem
The two-pointer problems (Move Zeroes, Reverse String, Container With Most Water) map to [[Two Pointers Sandwich Squeeze]] in card frontmatter
Each dish card preserves the user's actual code verbatim (e.g. the 'l, r' two-pointer swap, 'best = max(best, (r-l)' in Container)

evals.name: casual-small-batch-3-hashmap
assertions: All 3 problems get a dish-card markdown file under '03 - Dishes - Problems/' with an LC-numbered filename
An interactive HTML memory-palace file is produced under '04 - Meal Plans - Practice/'
The palace renders a flip-card for every one of the 3 problems, each back showing that problem's code
The palace organizes the dishes into at least one named, themed meal/room (a single hash-map room is acceptable for one shared theme)
A rhyming mnemonic poem with at least 3 dish lines appears, and it is present in BOTH the palace and the meal note
A meal note links every dish card, embeds the poem, and points to the palace HTML file
Every dish has a distinct exaggerated edible image name plus a one-line mnemonic, not a literal restatement of the problem
All three problems map to [[Hash Map Pantry Labels]] in card frontmatter
Each dish card preserves the user's actual code verbatim (e.g. 'seen[target-n]', 'len(set(nums))', 'Counter(s) == Counter(t)')
